calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Sep-30-2024 Dismissal - No Appearance

Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Appear At Deposition;Defendants Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Sep-20-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. MICHELLE JEW, Esq. of Law Offices of Johnson, Alvarez, Zakarian, Ferraris & Cadena (tel: 415-713-9186) for Defendants.
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT BASILIO TINIACOS HUERTA, AND NATANA ALVES' Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Appear At Deposition;Defendants Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Failure To Prosecute.

The Court notes Defendants are contesting the tentative ruling and do not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. The Court inquires Counsel Michelle Jew as to why she is not present in-person. The Court does not allow Counsel Michelle Jew to present oral argument, as in-person appearances are required if a party contests the tentative ruling or does not stipulate to the Judge Pro Tem. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Denied without prejudice. Defendants' notice of motion is inadequate under Rule 3.1110(b)(1) of the California Rules of Court.

The prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and email to the Judge Pro Tem at aaron@minnisandsmallets.com .

Judge: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (525/JPT)

Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Appear At Deposition;Defendants Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Failure To Prosecute
Off Calendar Aug-19-2024 Continued To Sep-20-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 525
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Michelle D. Jew, ESQ., LAW OFFICES OF JOHNSON, ALVAREZ, ZAKARIAN, FERRARIS & CADENA 415-281-7107., michelle.jew@allstate.com for Defendant.
No Other Appearances.

Court and counsel discuss the upcoming trial date. After discussion, The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BASILIO TINIACOS HUERTA AND NATANA ALVES' Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Appear At Deposition;Defendants Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff For Failure To Prosecute.

Transferred to the discovery calendar to be heard on September 20, 2024, at 9:00 a.m.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Answer Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Admissions; And Declaration Of Michelle D. Jew And Exhibits
Off Calendar Jan-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS BASILIO TINIACOS HUERTA, NATANA ALVES's Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiff To Answer Interrogatories, Request For Production Of Documents, Request For Admissions.

Defendants have filed a motion to compel responses by plaintiff to form and special interrogatories, a request for document production and a request for admissions. Meet and confer efforts were unsuccessful based on the representation by plaintiff's counsel that he was having difficulties reaching his client. For that reason, plaintiff's counsel served responses consisting only of objections.
Accordingly, IT IS HEREBY ORDERED that plaintiff's boilerplate objections are overruled. On or before January 31, 2024, plaintiff shall serve verified answers (without objections) to each and every discovery item propounded to him by defendants.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed form of order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at psw@hassard.com

Judge Pro Tem: Philip Ward, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/jpt)
